metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Mobility-as-a-Service domain, spanning multimodal access, payments, operator controls, and partner integration.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productExperience: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

experiences.name: Find the Right Parking Option
experiences.description: Help a driver or citizen understand parking options, rules, and availability before arrival.

flows.title: Find the Right Parking Option
flows.description: Help a driver or citizen understand parking options, rules, and availability before arrival.

steps.title: Search nearby parking
keyFacts: This step shapes first relevance and perceived product quality.
Users decide quickly whether the marketplace understands their intent.
painPoints: Low relevance or weak filtering creates early abandonment.
Users lose trust when the result set feels noisy or poorly explained.

dependencies.name: Parking Configuration & Policy Management
dependencies.description: Configures parking zones, tariffs, permits, exemptions, dwell rules, and city or operator policies across assets and channels.
dependencies.how: Uses Parking Configuration & Policy Management for parking zones, tariffs, permits, exemptions, dwell rules, and city or operator policies across assets and channels in search nearby parking.

dependencies.name: Reservations & Permit Inventory
dependencies.description: Handles reservable inventory, recurring permits, visitor parking, and premium access products.
dependencies.how: Uses Reservations & Permit Inventory for handles reservable inventory, recurring permits, visitor parking, and premium access products in search nearby parking.

steps.title: Compare parking policies
keyFacts: This step supports user visibility, decision quality, and operational control.
Users need concise signals, not raw system output.
painPoints: Users struggle when the state is hard to interpret or act on.
Decision quality drops when key signals are delayed or buried.

steps.title: Create planning scenario
keyFacts: This step determines how quickly users reach initial value.
It often combines setup effort, policy choices, and correctness checks.
painPoints: Users stall when setup asks for too much context too early.
Errors here create rework and reduce confidence in the product model.
